prompt_text: What are the best superfoods for heart health and energy? I can only shop at a food pantry that mostly provides canned goods and dry grains.
response_text:
Good news—you can build heart-healthy, energizing meals from pantry staples:

**Canned goods to prioritize:**
- **Beans** (black, kidney, pinto) - fiber, protein, minerals
- **Fish** (sardines, canned salmon) - omega-3s are excellent for heart health
- **Tomatoes** - lycopene and potassium
- **Vegetables** (carrots, green beans, peas) - nutrients and fiber

**Dry grains:**
- **Oats** - soluble fiber helps cholesterol
- **Brown rice** - B vitamins for energy
- **Whole wheat pasta** - sustained energy
- **Lentils/split peas** - protein + fiber combo

**Practical combinations:**
- Bean + grain meals (rice and beans, lentil soup)
- Canned fish on whole grain toast
- Oatmeal with canned fruit if available

**Budget tip:** Beans and grains together create complete proteins, so you get more nutritional value per dollar.

**One caution:** Canned goods can be high in sodium. If you have access to rinsing water, draining and rinsing canned beans/vegetables reduces salt by ~40%.

Does your pantry sometimes have fresh items, or access to any frozen vegetables? That would add options. Also worth asking pantry staff if they have whole grain options—some do.

extracted.summary: The response provides a list of heart-healthy superfoods that can be sourced from a food pantry, focusing on canned goods and dry grains.
caveats_and_disclaimers: Canned goods can be high in sodium. Rinsing can reduce salt.
